rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
PETITION TO TERMINATE ESTATE (NO ASSETS)
rulings.Rulings: Continued to February 25, 2014. Letter received from Administrator indicating that in his position as Administrator, he took control of assets and distributed them. The Administrator's interpretation of California law is not correct. No distribution of assets from an estate is permissible without a court order and his duties are not ended. Alternative number 1 - As there was property in the estate, an Inventory and Appraisal must be filed and a Petition for Final Distribution must be filed. Alternative number 2 - the surviving spouse can file a Spousal Property Petition. Once that petition is granted, the estate can be closed for lack of assets. Please note, one of these alternatives must be filed no later than February 19, 2014. If neither is filed by that date, an appearance by the Administrator will be required on February 25, 2014.
